LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DEBORAH FARRINGTON, JEANNE SULLIVAN, STARVEST PARTNERS II, L.P., STARVEST PARTNERS II (PARALLEL), L.P., AND, STARVEST PARTNERS II (ADVISORS), L.P. MOTION TO STAY THE COMPLAINT ON THE GROUND OF INCONVENIENT FORUM. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND AMENDED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION TO STAY IS GRANTED. NEW YORK IS A SUITABLE ALTERNATIVE FORUM. ALL CALIFORNIA DEFENDANTS STIPULATE TO NEW YORK JURISDICTION. NEW YORK WAS THE FIRST FILED SUIT AND DISCOVERY IS PROGRESSING. PLAINTIFF FILED AN ANSWER IN THE NEW YORK ACTION, CONSENTING TO JURISDICTION. PLAINTIFF MAY FILE A CROSS-COMPLAINT FOR DAMAGES IN THE NEW YORK ACTION. ALSO, STAY COULD BE LIFTED DEPENDING ON THE OUTCOME IN THE NEW YORK COURT. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Notice Of Motion To Stay The Complaint The Defts On The Ground Of Inconveniet Forum
Off Calendar Jul-16-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT DEBORAH FARRINGTON, JEANNE SULLIVAN, STARVEST PARTNERS II, L.P., STARVEST PARTNERS II (PARALLEL), L.P., and, STARVEST PARTNERS II (ADVISORS), L.P. Motion To Stay The Complaint On The Ground Of Inconvenient Forum. Appearances: Kirk B. Freeman, Esq. Law Offices of Kirk B. Freeman, (415) 398-1082, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; John C. Ertman, Esq. & Thad Davis, Esq. of Ropes & Gray (212) 841-0669, appearing for Defendants and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to stay is granted. New York is a suitable alternative forum. All California defendants stipulate to New York jurisdiction. New York was the first filed suit and discovery is progressing. Plaintiff filed an answer in the New York action, consenting to jurisdiction. Plaintiff may file a cross-complaint for damages in the New York action. Also, Stay could be lifted depending on the outcome in the New York Court. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar Jul-16-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT DEBORAH FARRINGTON, JEANNE SULLIVAN, STARVEST PARTNERS II, L.P., STARVEST PARTNERS II (PARALLEL), L.P., STARVEST PARTNERS II (ADVISORS), L.P. MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE. John C. Ertman, Esq. and Thad Davis, Esq. of Ropes & Gray (212) 841-0669, appearing for Defendants and moving party. This motion is not being contested. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to admit John C. Ertman, as counsel pro hac vice is granted. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
